items.summary: This pull request addresses a bug in the OpenClaw project related to OAuth profile expiry display. Previously, the system incorrectly showed stale expiry information for auto-renewable OAuth profiles, such as displaying "expires in 0m" despite the token being set to auto-renew. The fix involves clearing the `expiresAt` and `remainingMs` fields when the profile's status is overridden to "ok" (indicating auto-renewal), ensuring that downstream renderers do not display misleading expiry times. This update improves the accuracy of OAuth token status representation, preventing contradictory information in the user interface.

items.summary: This pull request addresses an issue in the Mattermost integration within the openclaw project, where multi-turn responses involving tool calls were not streaming correctly. Specifically, during in-place streaming of multi-turn interactions, the same message content was improperly replaced when a tool call occurred, leading to confusing or inconsistent user experiences. The update ensures that each turn in a multi-tool call response is streamed independently, preventing content replacement and improving clarity. This fix enhances the reliability of streaming multi-turn conversations in Mattermost, especially when multiple tool calls are involved, by handling message updates more accurately during live interactions.

items.summary: The GitHub issue reports a timeout problem with the `openclaw models status --probe` command on version 2026.3.8. The user experienced a request timeout (~8.4 seconds) when probing the openai-codex/gpt-5.2 model, despite valid OAuth authentication. The issue includes warnings about unknown entries in the tools profile and notes that some users had to manually roll back after upgrading. This suggests a potential bug or performance issue introduced in the latest release, possibly affecting model probing stability.

items.summary: The GitHub issue reports a regression bug in OpenClaw version 2026.3.8 where clicking the "Run Now" button on a cron job with `sessionTarget: isolated` returns a success response but the task does not execute. Despite the API indicating the task was enqueued (`ok: true, enqueued: true`), no task runs and no entry appears in the `cron/runs/` directory. The issue prevents immediate execution of scheduled jobs via the UI, indicating a failure in triggering or processing the task despite successful API acknowledgment. This bug impacts the reliability of manual cron job execution in OpenClaw and requires further investigation to restore expected behavior.

- Created: 2026-03-10T11:48:27Z - Updated: 2026-03-10T12:08:58Z ### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary Cron jobs created in 2026.3.8 transition "running" → "idle" but never write run history. The "finished" event is not  ...    ### Steps to reproduce

# 1. Create a simple cron job openclaw cron add --name "Test Cron Bug" --at "2m" --session main --system-event "Run: uptime && free -h"  ...    • Gateway logs show "FailoverError: LLM request timed out" but no "finished" event  ...    ### OpenClaw version

2026.3.8 (3caab92)

### Operating system

Linux 6.8.12-19-pve (Proxmox LXC container) ### Install method

npm install -g openclaw (official release)

### Model google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite (tested) minimax-m2.1:cloud (tested)

### Provider / routing chain OpenClaw Gateway → Ollama Cloud (openai-completions adapter) → Gemini/Minimax

### Config file / key location  ...    Affected users: All users creating new cron automations after 2026.3.8 Severity: HIGH — Blocks core automation functionality

Frequency: Always reproducible with any new cron job Consequences:
• Cannot audit cron job execution (no run history) • Scheduler becomes unresponsive when jobs stick in "running" (~60 min)
• No visibility into job failures or timeouts  ...    Business impact: Cron scheduling is a core OpenClaw feature. This regression blocks users from creating reliable new automations. ### Additional information Regression confirmed:
• Last known good: Pre-2026.3.8 jobs work perfectly
• First known bad: 2026.3.7 / 2026.3.8  ...    Suspected root cause:The "FailoverError: LLM request timed out" error handling path in cron lane execution skips emitting the "finished" event, so appendCronRunLog() 
is never called. ---

### Timeline

**z3ldox** added label `bug` · Mar 10, 2026 at 11:48am **openclaw-barnacle[bot]** added label `regression` · Mar 10, 2026 at 11:48am  ...    > Conclusion: Cron lane has single execution slot. When one job hangs, others queue behind it (queueAhead=5). Jobs  ...    **z3ldox** changed the title from "[BUG 2026.3.8] Cron jobs complete (running→idle) but run history not written - no "finished" event emitted" to "[BUG 2026.3.8] Cron lane backlog: jobs queue behind stuck runs (queueAhead=5, wait 60m+) causing scheduler starvation" · Mar 10, 2026 at 12:06pm  ...    > ## UPDATE: Root Cause Refined
> 
> **Issue is NOT "run history missing"** — it's **cron lane backlog/starvation**. >

>

>

items.summary: This GitHub issue reports a regression bug in openclaw version 2026.3.8, where cron jobs transition from "running" to "idle" but fail to log their run history due to the "finished" event not being emitted after an LLM timeout. This prevents the `appendCronRunLog()` function from executing, leading to a backlog of jobs behind stuck runs, causing scheduler starvation. The issue was identified after creating and manually running a cron job, with the problem manifesting approximately 30 seconds later. The bug impacts the scheduling and logging of cron jobs, potentially affecting system reliability and performance. The issue is currently open and labeled as a bug and regression, indicating it worked correctly in previous versions and now requires attention for a fix.

items.summary: This pull request addresses a security issue where provider authentication environment variables (e.g., `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`, `HF_TOKEN`) could inadvertently leak into child ACP processes. The fix involves stripping these sensitive environment variables from the environment passed to child processes, enhancing security by preventing credential exposure. The update includes 597 additions and 115 deletions across 19 files, and it was merged to improve the robustness of the system's environment handling.

items.summary: The GitHub issue reports a regression in OpenClaw version 2026.3.8, following an upgrade from 2026.3.7. It causes intermittent failures in agent responses, including empty payloads with stop reasons like "stop," "length," and "error" (notably "LLM request timed out"). Additionally, there are websocket closures with code 1006. These issues occur even with minimal prompts and disabled thinking. Reverting to version 2026.3.7 resolves the problems. The bug is classified as a regression, indicating a recent change introduced instability.

items.summary: This pull request addresses a Windows-specific issue where the `openclaw update` command could fail due to file lock conflicts caused by the gateway service being active during self-update operations. The failure manifests as an `npm ERR! EBUSY` error during the rename process, preventing successful updates and leaving users stuck on outdated versions. The fix involves modifying the update process to prevent or handle file locks more gracefully, ensuring smoother self-updates on Windows systems. The change includes four commits, adding 393 lines and removing 95, and was merged to improve reliability and user experience during updates.

items.title: Bug: `openclaw backup create` stalls — produces 10-byte .tmp file and never completes · Issue #41830 · openclaw/openclaw
highlights:
openclaw/openclaw #41830  ...    | TypeScript ## Bug: `openclaw backup create` stalls — produces 10-byte.tmp file and never completes - Author: @bo-blue
- State: open
- Created: 2026-03-10T06:16:55Z
- Updated: 2026-03-10T06:16:55Z ## Reproduction Run `openclaw backup create --output ~/some-dir` (directory must be outside `~/.openclaw/` or you get a source-path error). **Expected:** A completed `.tar.gz` archive in the output directory. **Actual:** A 10-byte `.tmp` file is created and the process returns with no output and no error. The `.tmp` file is never renamed to the final archive. ```
$ openclaw backup create --output ~/Desktop/openclaw-backups
(no output, exits 0)
$ ls ~/Desktop/openclaw-backups/  ...    2026-03-10T05-44-55.584Z-openclaw-backup.tar.gz.65184d2d-85fa-4b88-be27-456cd3e2932d.tmp  ...    Also reproduced with `--verify` flag. Also tried `--only-config` — same result.

## Environment - OpenClaw: 2026.3.8 (3caab92)
- OS: macOS 26.3.1 (arm64)
- Node: 25.6.1

## Notes The `--dry-run --json` flag works correctly (prints the plan). The issue is in the archive write/finalization step. The backup was added in this release so this appears to be a regression or incomplete implementation in the initial ship. *Filed by bo-blue on behalf of XOGO deployment.*

---

### Timeline **ywxc1997** mentioned this in PR #1: fix(backup): treat non-existent output paths as directories · Mar 10, 2026 at 6:58am **ywxc1997** mentioned this in PR #41868: fix(backup): treat non-existent output paths as directories · Mar  ...    **ywxc1997** mentioned this in PR [#42052: fix(backup):
items.summary: The GitHub issue describes a bug in openclaw where the `backup create` command stalls, creating only a 10-byte temporary file instead of completing the backup archive. This issue occurs when specifying an output directory outside `~/.openclaw/`. The process exits without errors or output, leaving a small `.tmp` file that is never renamed to the final `.tar.gz` archive. The problem affects backup creation, impacting reliability and performance. The issue is currently open, with no resolution provided yet. Users experiencing this should verify directory paths and monitor for updates or fixes in future releases.

items.summary: This pull request addresses a bug in the `sessions_spawn` function of the openclaw project, where subagents incorrectly inherited the caller's workspace instead of their own. Specifically, when spawning a `browser` agent, the subagent was using the main agent's workspace directory (`/root/.openclaw/workspace`) instead of its dedicated workspace (`/root/.openclaw/workspace-browser`). The fix ensures that each subagent inherits its own target agent workspace, improving isolation and correctness in agent spawning.

items.title: [Bug]: When `fetchUser("@me")` fails during Discord provider startup (e.g. due to transient network issues after a proxy reconnect), the bot continues running with `botUserId = undefined`. This causes multiple security and routing failures. · Issue #42219 · openclaw/openclaw

- Created: 2026-03-10T14:20:42Z - Updated: 2026-03-10T14:20:55Z ### Bug type

### Summary

### Impact 1. **Mention gating bypassed**: The guard `if (botId && mentionGate.shouldSkip)` in `message-handler.preflight.ts:686`  ...    2. **Self-message filtering disabled**: `author.id === botUserId` (line 160) never matches, risking self-reply loops.  ...    In `provider.ts:656-662`, `fetchUser("@me")` failure is caught and only logged — the bot proceeds to accept messages with `botUserId = undefined`: ```typescript
try {
    const botUser = await client.fetchUser("@me");
    botUserId = botUser?.id;
} catch (err) { runtime.error?.(danger(`discord: failed to fetch bot identity: ${String(err)}`));  ...    By contrast, `fetchDiscordApplicationId` (line 407-410) correctly throws on failure, preventing startup. ### Suggested Fix

Two layers: 1. **Fail-fast**: Throw when `botUserId` is not resolved, so the provider restarts instead of running in a degraded  ...    1. Configure a Discord channel with `requireMention: true` 2. Start the gateway in an environment where `fetchUser("@me")` fails (e.g. transient proxy/network issue) 3. Observe `logged in to discord` (without bot ID) in logs  ...    If `fetchUser("@me")` fails, the provider should throw and let auto-restart retry, matching the existing pattern for  ...    ### OpenClaw version

- openclaw: 2026.3.9 - Node: 22+

macOS 15.4

items.summary: This GitHub issue reports a regression bug in the openclaw/openclaw project where, during Discord provider startup, a failure in `fetchUser("@me")`—such as due to transient network issues after a proxy reconnect—causes the bot to continue running with an undefined `botUserId`. This leads to critical security and routing failures, including bypassing mention gating and disabling self-message filtering, which can cause security vulnerabilities and message handling issues. The bug is marked as a security concern and is currently open, highlighting the need for improved error handling during startup to prevent the bot from operating in an insecure or inconsistent state.
